name: Anzu Robotics
parent_entity: XTI Drones Holdings (subsidiary of XTI Aerospace, NASDAQ: XTIA)
holding_company: The Origin Group AZ, Inc. (Delaware corporation, pre-acquisition)
headquarters: 6500 River Place Blvd, Building 7, Suite 2, Austin, TX
license_details: Licensed specs and SDK from DJI Mavic 3 Enterprise. DJI receives per-unit payment. Warnas told NYT arrangement was essentially DJI's idea. DJI confirmed in Aug 2024 blog: standard and legal arrangement.

acquisition.concurrent_investor: Unusual Machines Inc — $25M in XTI preferred stock

anzu_compliance_statement: Explicitly stated: Not Blue UAS, not NDAA compliant, has Chinese components
china_component_detail: ~50% from China. DJI P1 OcuSync chipset (proprietary). Thermal sensor from China. DJI batteries backward-compatible.
data_hosting_provider: Aloft Technologies (Syracuse, NY — ISO 27001 / SOC 2 Type II)

security_architecture.firmware_signing: DJI cryptographic keys — DJI retains root key control
security_architecture.sdk: DJI SDK — Aloft app built on it
security_architecture.key_chipset: DJI P1 OcuSync ASIC — cannot be independently sourced
security_architecture.pentest: White Knight Labs May 2024 — data to Aloft only, Chinese domain refs remediated

fcc_filings.initial_disclosure: Did NOT disclose DJI relationship — disclosed only after researchers went public

security_researchers.name: Ian Lewis (Mads Tech)
security_researchers.contribution: Hardware teardowns — identical PCBs to DJI Mavic 3

security_researchers.name: Konrad Iturbe
security_researchers.contribution: Firmware analysis, documented 9+ suspected DJI shell companies

misleading_origin_claims: Your solution is delivered by your neighbors
voluntary_disclosures: Stated not Blue UAS and not NDAA compliant
Said 'If NDAA compliance is required we may not be the right product'

pricing_context.vs_blue_uas: Skydio X10D $15K-20K+, Inspired Flight IF800 $25K+, BRINC Lemur $12K+

known_government_buyers.funding: Arkansas Public Safety Equipment Grant (SOAR program)
known_government_buyers.detail: City Board of Directors approved resolution. Established patrol-based drone response team.
Officer Jake House: 'What used to take 20 police officers to do, now I can do with two and a drone.' Uses for crime scenes, car crashes, missing kids, SWAT overwatch, K9 deployments.
Integrated Anzu Raptor T into drone program. Referenced in ABJ Academy coverage.

legal_actions.court: District Court of Collin County, Texas
legal_actions.case_context: Second of four CCP-aligned lawsuits that week (TP-Link, Lorex, Temu)
legal_actions.core_allegation: DJI drones in all but name — identical hardware, firmware, software, only green paint and American label
legal_actions.key_technical_claim: DJI retains root cryptographic keys — technical capability to access/modify/extract data
relief_sought: Civil penalties up to $10K per violation
legal_actions.status: pending — no public response from Anzu as of April 2026
legal_actions.other_states: No other state AG has filed similar action

congressional_scrutiny.key_conclusion: Raptor T is essentially a DJI Mavic 3 painted green
congressional_scrutiny.warnas_response: Public statement only — no substantive response to 12 questions surfaced

congressional_scrutiny.action: Requested national security review of Chinese drone manufacturers

defenders: sUAS News: 'We are on team Anzu'
Industry: Warnas was more transparent than most
Some view ban effort as protectionism for Skydio/BRINC
critics: Researchers: transparency about licensing != transparency about DJI root keys
Warnas 'I like to be unaware' undermines independence claims

timeline.event: Warnas YouTube: DJI execs asked 'what can we do?'
Researchers publish teardown — identical PCBs, DJI crypto keys
DJI publishes 'Get The Facts' confirming license
Little Rock PD purchases 6 Raptor T
NDAA Section 1709 / FCC Covered List take effect
Texas AG lawsuit filed — 7 DTPA counts

notes: Canonical gray zone case. Warnas was more transparent than Cogito/SkyRover but the gap between 'we licensed DJI tech' and 'DJI retains root crypto keys' is the core issue. NDAA Section 1709 explicitly covers licensing agreements — arguably written for this exact scenario. Randall is known in the industry and was upfront about many things — the community is divided on whether he was a bad actor or just caught in a regulatory vise.
flags_for_deeper_research: Full list of government buyers beyond 3 documented
Texas lawsuit status — has Anzu responded?
Warnas formal responses to Committee's 12 questions

KARK: N. Little Rock & Little Rock PD Anzu Raptor T Deployment

latest_developments.tx_lawsuit_status: Filed Feb 18, 2026 in Collin County District Court. 7 counts DTPA. No public response from Anzu as of April 2026. DroneLife attempted contact — no response. Lawsuit frozen 45+ days.
latest_developments.xti_acquisition_update: XTI acquired Anzu + Drone Nerds Nov 10, 2025 for ~$41.6M total ($20M cash, $11.9M notes, $9.7M Class B units). XTI secured $20M JPMorgan ABL facility Feb 11, 2026 with Anzu as co-borrower. XTI delayed 10-K filing (April 2026) due to complex purchase price allocations. Sold legacy Inpixon RTLS business Feb 2026.
latest_developments.fcc_impact: All foreign-made drones added to FCC Covered List Dec 22, 2025. Anzu Raptor already discontinued Feb 10, 2026. FCC ban renders shell company strategy moot.
latest_developments.product_status: Zero products for sale. No successor timeline disclosed. No proprietary technology.
latest_developments.competitive_position: Skydio >$700M raised. Well-capitalized competitors have sovereign supply chains. Anzu has zero-product gap with customer churn accelerating.
latest_developments.randall_warnas_status: No public statements since lawsuit filing. Previously CEO/sole employee.
latest_developments.umac_xti_nexus: NOTE: UMAC is NOT a shell company — it is a legitimate NYSE-listed US drone manufacturer (Fat Shark, Rotor Riot). Unusual Machines (NYSE:UMAC) invested $25M in XTI Series 10 Convertible Preferred at $1.492 conversion — concurrent with Anzu acquisition. UMAC is NDAA-compliant US drone component manufacturer. CEO Allan Evans cited 'unprecedented opportunity to define domestic drone landscape as US intensifies ban on Chinese companies.' UMAC raised $157.8M equity in 2025, targeting cash-flow positive by end 2026. 2025 revenue $11.2M (101% YoY). $103.3M cash. Needham Buy target $20. UMAC also has $3.75M PDW AM-FPV order and 101st Airborne supply agreements.
latest_developments.drone_nerds_context: Drone Nerds: largest US drone distributor. Revenue >$100M in 2024, >$55M through June 2025. 10-year profitability record. This is the real asset in the XTI acquisition — Anzu was the strategic play, Drone Nerds is the revenue engine.

passthrough_legal_theory: TX AG Paxton's case tests 'passthrough theory': whether licensing, rebranding, or supply-chain ties to a scrutinized foreign manufacturer can constitute deceptive practices under state consumer protection law. If successful, precedent could apply to all gray zone entities. If injunction granted, state agencies and local governments could be forced to pause/reexamine Anzu contracts.

name: Cogito Tech / SPECTA
license_details: NO acknowledgment of DJI. Identical PCBs to DJI Air 3, same P1 chipset, RF emission reports differ only by date/logo removal, firmware forked from DJI with branding stripped.

congressional_scrutiny.action: Named alongside Anzu in letter to Commerce Secretary

timeline.event: Specta Air appears — identified as DJI Air 3 clone
Named in congressional letter to Commerce

notes: Less transparent than Anzu — zero DJI acknowledgment, no cybersecurity disclosure. Targets consumer market. Establishes the white-label playbook others replicate.

latest_developments.fcc_status: FCC filings under COGITO TECH COMPANY LIMITED (Hong Kong). SPECTA Air and GL3323 both filed. OcuSync frequency fingerprint matches DJI Air 3.
latest_developments.fcc_covered_list_impact: All foreign-made drones on Covered List as of Dec 22, 2025. Cogito products cannot receive new FCC authorizations.
latest_developments.shell_company_detection: Identified by Konrad Iturbe's automated OcuSync detection system. Part of 12+ company network using DJI proprietary communication protocols.
latest_developments.product_status: SPECTA Air still listed on various retail platforms. Identical PCBs to DJI Air 3 confirmed by teardown analysis.
latest_developments.corporate_details: Hong Kong-based. FCC registration Aug 2023. Website built with Google Sites — compliance/security/contact links broken. No support infrastructure. Warranty via Amazon only. Denies any DJI connection but OcuSync frequencies, identical PCBs, and patented DJI chipsets prove otherwise.
latest_developments.product_details: SPECTA Air = DJI Air 3 (identical PCBs, batteries within 2g, same firmware fork). SPECTA Mini = DJI Mini 2 SE/4K. Manufactured in Malaysia. Sold primarily via Amazon. $1,199 for Air (vs $1,099 DJI Air 3, but includes RC2-equiv controller). Half Chrome teardown: 'It IS the Air 3, inside and out.'
latest_developments.key_researchers: Half Chrome (Jack Towne) — full teardown. Ian Lewis (Mads Tech) — DJI patented chipset identification. Konrad Iturbe — OcuSync frequency detection. Kevin Finisterre (@d0tslash) — firmware analysis confirming DJI involvement.

name: SkyRover / Knowact
legal_name: SZ Knowact Robot Technology Co., Ltd.
license_details: App connects directly to DJI cloud. Identical encryption keys. DJI login credentials work. Zero corporate transparency. Layered structure for brand rotation if banned.

shell_company_network.pattern: FCC apps — Spatial Hover docs contained DJI logos

timeline.event: SkyRover X1 appears — DJI Mini 4 Pro clone identified
DroneXL exposure: DJI infrastructure links confirmed

notes: Next-gen gray zone entity — emerged after Anzu drew scrutiny. Zero transparency, direct DJI cloud, layered corporate structure for resilience. 9+ suspected DJI shells documented.
flags_for_deeper_research: FCC action on shell company apps

FCC Filing: SkyRover X1 RC (Skyhigh Tech)
Konrad Iturbe: DJI Front Companies Tracker

Apple App Store: SKYROVER app by Knowact

latest_developments.fcc_status: FCC filings list SZ Knowact Robot Technology Co., Ltd (Shenzhen) as company of record. SkyRover X1 RC filed under Skyhigh Tech LLC. All subject to FCC Covered List.
latest_developments.us_manufacturing: SkyRover announced 5-year plan to 'explore US-based manufacturing' (March 2026). Plans vague — 'explore' not 'commit'.
latest_developments.shell_company_network: Konrad Iturbe's automated OcuSync frequency detection bot identified 12+ companies selling DJI-based drones. WaveGo Tech FCC filing included Knowact documentation. DJI logos still visible in Skyhigh Tech FCC antenna diagrams.
latest_developments.retail_status: SkyRover drones still available on Amazon and at retailers. Spring 2026 sales: S1 bundle $359, X1 $599.
latest_developments.dji_shell_gambit_impact: DroneXL analysis: shell company strategy 'backfired spectacularly' — provoked FCC to ban ALL foreign drones, not just DJI. 'The response was predictable to anyone who understands how Washington actually works.'

Fikaxo Technology Inc — products migrated to Spatial Hover overnight
Spatial Hover Inc — appeared Sept 30, 2025, 4 days after DroneXL exposed network

name: Autel Robotics
legal_name: Autel Robotics Co., Ltd. / Autel Robotics USA LLC
license_details: Own technology — but openly Chinese-owned. Founded by Li Hongjing. Chinese govt 'Little Giant' designation. CITIC Corp (Ministry of Finance) among seed investors. Chinese website markets to 'public security, army, national defense, armed police.'
misleading_origin_claims: US subsidiary in Bothell creates impression of American company

regulatory_actions.action: Commerce Entity List — shipping controlled items to Russia
DoD Section 1260H Chinese Military Companies list

congressional_scrutiny.note: Named in NDAA, Entity List, Section 1260H

timeline.event: Warnas briefly CEO of NA subsidiary

notes: Different gray zone position — openly Chinese but marketed as DJI alternative to US law enforcement. Own IP unlike Anzu/Cogito/SkyRover. Warnas's 9-week Autel stint connects these entities.

latest_developments.fcc_covered_list: Named entity in FY2025 NDAA Section 1709 alongside DJI. Unlike generic foreign drone ban, Autel cannot bypass via US manufacturing or licensing — the ban follows the entity.
latest_developments.authorization_revocations: FCC quietly revoked certain DJI/Autel authorizations in Jan 2026 that were issued shortly before Dec 22, 2025 Covered List update. No public notice of which devices.
latest_developments.legal_challenge: DJI filed Ninth Circuit appeal (Case 26-1029). FCC moved to dismiss on ripeness grounds. Autel legal posture unclear but faces same Section 1709 restrictions.
latest_developments.fcc_conditional_approvals: First 4 non-Chinese drones removed from Covered List March 18, 2026 (SiFly, Mobilicom, ScoutDI, Verge). No Chinese-manufactured system has been cleared. DoW 45-day review took 70 days.
latest_developments.market_impact: DJI+Autel commanded 70%+ of US market. 80%+ of 1,800+ law enforcement agencies used DJI. Replacement costs 2-10x higher.
latest_developments.legal_posture: Autel has been quieter than DJI. The Wiley Law roundup header says 'DJI and Autel Challenge Their Covered List Designations' but detail focuses on DJI's Ninth Circuit filing. Autel appears to be seeking reconsideration at FCC level but no separate federal court challenge confirmed. Autel faces identical Section 1709 restrictions — cannot bypass even via US manufacturing.
latest_developments.fcc_revocations: FCC OET quietly revoked certain Autel authorizations in Jan 2026 that were issued shortly before Dec 22 Covered List. No public notice of which specific devices. TCBs were informed privately.
